{
  "body": "**Uberon term**\nUBERON:0001629\ncarotid body\n\n**Suggested revision and reasons**\n\nThe current parentage of this term indicate that it is part of \"common carotid artery plus branches\" (UBERON:0001530), which is incorrect. \n\nAccording to PMID:[32965908](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32965908), and according to the current definition, \"carotid body is a sensory organ in the peripheral nervous system located in the adventitia of the bifurcation of the common carotid artery\". \nWhile the structure is \"adjacent to\", it is not \"part of: the  \"common carotid artery plus branches\" \n\nAccording to the information above, this term should be part of the peripheral nervous system.",
  "comments": [
    {
      "id": "IC_kwDOAAidd869y9qu",
      "author": {
        "login": "cmungall"
      },
      "authorAssociation": "MEMBER",
      "body": "@dragon-ai-agent please fix this.",

      "id": "IC_kwDOAAidd869zF1K",
      "author": {
        "login": "dragon-ai-agent"
      },
      "authorAssociation": "COLLABORATOR",
      "body": "## Issue Fixed ✅\n\nI have successfully reclassified the carotid body (UBERON:0001629) to correctly place it within the peripheral nervous system rather than the cardiovascular system.\n\n### Changes Made:\n- **Relationship corrected**: Removed \n- **New relationship**: Added  \n- **Evidence added**: Included PMID:32965908 reference to support the classification\n- **Issue tracking**: Added proper metadata linking to this issue (#3591)\n\n### Scientific Rationale:\nThe carotid body is functionally and developmentally a sensory organ of the peripheral nervous system. While anatomically located adjacent to the carotid artery bifurcation, it is not structurally part of the cardiovascular system. The previous classification was based on location rather than the organ's true biological function and developmental origin.\n\n### Next Steps:\n- **Pull Request**: #3594 is ready for review\n- **Branch**: \n- **Validation**: All ontology QA checks have passed\n\nThe fix ensures the carotid body is properly classified according to its role as a chemoreceptive sensory organ within the peripheral nervous system, as supported by the referenced literature.\n\n**@dragon-ai-agent**",
      "createdAt": "2025-08-13T14:59:54Z",
      "includesCreatedEdit": false,
      "isMinimized": false,
      "minimizedReason": "",

  "title": "\"carotid body\" should not be part of the cardiovascular system",

name: carotid body
def: "A small epithelioid structure consisting of a small cluster of chemoreceptive and supporting cells located near the bifurcation of the common carotid artery that serves as a chemoreceptive organ that senses the pH, carbon dioxide, and oxygen concentrations in the blood and plays a crucial role in their homeostatic control." git add src/ontology/uberon-edit.obo && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Fix carotid body (UBERON:0001629) parentage - issue #3591

The carotid body was previously incorrectly classified as part_of
UBERON:0001530 (common carotid artery plus branches). The carotid body
is a chemoreceptive sensory organ of the peripheral nervous system that
is located adjacent to (in the adventitia of) the bifurcation of the
common carotid artery, but it is not anatomically part of the
cardiovascular system.

Per PMID:32965908 and the existing definition, the carotid body is a
sensory organ in the peripheral nervous system. The part_of relationship
has been re-targeted from UBERON:0001530 to UBERON:0000010 (peripheral
nervous system). The existing 'overlaps' relationships to the internal
and external carotid arteries are retained to preserve information about
the anatomical adjacency to the carotid vessels.

A term_tracker_item linking back to obophenotype/uberon#3591 has been
added.

Signed-off-by: @dragon-ai-agent

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git status

input.content:
# Fix carotid body (UBERON:0001629) parentage

Addresses obophenotype/uberon#3591.

## Problem

`carotid body` (UBERON:0001629) was previously asserted to be `part_of` `common carotid artery plus branches` (UBERON:0001530). This is anatomically incorrect: the carotid body is a chemoreceptive sensory organ located *in the adventitia of* the carotid bifurcation, i.e. adjacent to (not part of) the artery. The term's own textual definition already says it is "located near the bifurcation of the common carotid artery", and PMID:32965908 explicitly describes it as a sensory organ in the peripheral nervous system.

## Change

In UBERON:0001629 (`carotid body`):

- Removed: `relationship: part_of UBERON:0001530 ! common carotid artery plus branches`
- Added:   `relationship: part_of UBERON:0000010 {source="PMID:32965908"} ! peripheral nervous system`
- Added:   `property_value: term_tracker_item "https://github.com/obophenotype/uberon/issues/3591" xsd:anyURI`

The existing `overlaps` relationships to the internal carotid artery (UBERON:0001532) and external carotid artery (UBERON:0001070) are intentionally retained — they correctly capture the anatomical adjacency of the carotid body to the carotid vessels without implying parthood.

The existing `is_a` to `nonchromaffin paraganglion` (UBERON:0034979) is also retained; that classification (and the upstream developmental link to neural crest via `paraganglion (generic)`) is fully consistent with the PNS parthood.

# Fix carotid body (UBERON:0001629) parentage

Addresses obophenotype/uberon#3591.

## Problem

`carotid body` (UBERON:0001629) was previously asserted to be `part_of` `common carotid artery plus branches` (UBERON:0001530). This is anatomically incorrect: the carotid body is a chemoreceptive sensory organ located *in the adventitia of* the carotid bifurcation, i.e. adjacent to (not part of) the artery. The term's own textual definition already says it is "located near the bifurcation of the common carotid artery", and PMID:32965908 explicitly describes it as a sensory organ in the peripheral nervous system.

## Change

In UBERON:0001629 (`carotid body`):

- Removed: `relationship: part_of UBERON:0001530 ! common carotid artery plus branches`
- Added:   `relationship: part_of UBERON:0000010 {source="PMID:32965908"} ! peripheral nervous system`
- Added:   `property_value: term_tracker_item "https://github.com/obophenotype/uberon/issues/3591" xsd:anyURI`

The existing `overlaps` relationships to the internal carotid artery (UBERON:0001532) and external carotid artery (UBERON:0001070) are intentionally retained — they correctly capture the anatomical adjacency of the carotid body to the carotid vessels without implying parthood.

The existing `is_a` to `nonchromaffin paraganglion` (UBERON:0034979) is also retained; that classification (and the upstream developmental link to neural crest via `paraganglion (generic)`) is fully consistent with the PNS parthood.
